checks.finding: All four tasks (T-001 line 56, T-002 line 199, T-003 line 328, T-004 line 468) declare Risk: medium. Each states 'Data Migration: none' and 'Breaking API: no', and each Risk Rationale grounds the medium tier in read-only invocation of real gates/validators on synthetic mktemp fixtures with 'no protected-file edit and no security surface change', matching risk-classification-policy.md's medium tier (line 15). No task's Scope/Done-When text modifies authentication, authorization, payment, PII storage, data migration, or a breaking/externally-visible API contract as an implementation target (only as a read-only test subject); loop-inventory/v1 is explicitly 'a new, additive registry contract... No existing script, gate, or artifact format changes' (T-001 lines 86-88). No task is high/critical with UI-only scope, so over-classification does not apply either. No issues found.

SKIP: no Risk: high or critical task exists in tasks.md — T-001 (line 56), T-002 (line 199), T-003 (line 328), and T-004 (line 468) are all Risk: medium, so the mandated Red→Green / independent-review-verdict / second-approver Done-When evidence items do not apply to this task set.

Done When lists: T-001 8 items (lines 145-176), T-002 7 items (lines 282-306), T-003 8 items (lines 416-444), T-004 8 items (lines 558-589) — none exceed the 8-item oversizing threshold. No task title implies sequential multi-phase work; each task pairs one deliverable with its own test suite, matching the Global Constraints 'one issue = one commit' convention (tasks.md line 28). No fragmentation: each task delivers a full suite plus supporting artifacts, not a single-function or single-file edit. No issues found.

Every task's linked ACs embed at least one edge/error-path scenario beyond the happy path: T-001/AC-001 negative self-check removing a registered entry from a mktemp inventory copy (acceptance-tests.md line 5); AC-002 mutated-cap red proof (line 6); T-002/AC-006 'a manifest referencing a nonexistent artifact fails' (line 10); AC-007 schema-mutation and terminal-contradiction negative self-checks (line 11); T-003/AC-009 RED differential against 2d8c6a5^ (line 13); AC-010 synthetic required-but-unauthorized manifest entry turning red (line 14); T-004/AC-013 python3-absent degradation path (line 17); AC-018 T-001/T-0010 prefix-collision fixture (line 22). No functional task relies on happy-path-only acceptance criteria. No issues found.

acceptance-tests.md's 'integration'-labeled rows each genuinely span ≥2 system components: TEST-005 (fixture init + REAL validate-review-context-set.sh hash-chain validation, line 9), TEST-006 (REAL precheck + REAL validator + reviewer-output generation via drive_review_round, line 10), TEST-008 (spec/impl/task/domain loop drivers plus inventory terminal comparison, line 12), TEST-011 (cycle-limit + select-agent-model + terminal-tier schema + resume script, line 15). All 18 test rows (lines 5-22) cite a specific AC-NNN in their own row. No row is mislabeled 'unit' for a multi-component scope or 'end-to-end' for a partial workflow. No issues found.

No task carries Risk: high or critical, so this check's mandatory trigger condition is absent (see RISK-APPROPRIATE). All four tasks nonetheless include explicit Rollback: fields: T-001 lines 90-92, T-002 lines 235-236, T-003 lines 362-363, T-004 lines 502-503, each stating 'revert this task's commit; nothing protected is touched, so no human-copy re-copy step exists in the rollback path,' consistent with infra-spec.md's Rollback section (lines 92-98), which also forbids deregistering a suite without deleting it (enforced by TEST-004). No issues found.

tasks.md T-003 (Planned Files, lines 347-352) and T-004 (Planned Files, lines 487-492) both list tests/run-all.sh, tests/run-all.ps1, and .github/workflows/test.yml for 'this suite's registration' edits, but neither task's Blockers field names the other (T-003 line 456: 'T-001, T-002'; T-004 line 600: 'T-001, T-002'). tasks.md Global Constraints (lines 32-37) requires 'commits on the single feature branch are serialized in task-ID order (T-003's registration commit lands before T-004's)', while design.md's Dependency Order section (lines 273-277) states 'A3 and A4' proceed 'in parallel' (addressing only fixture non-collision, not the shared-file commit ordering). Nothing in the task graph forces T-003 before T-004 on these three shared files, so an implementer following only Blockers could stage T-004's registration edits first, contradicting the Global Constraints' serialization promise and risking a same-file registration conflict.

tasks.md T-004's Blockers field (line 600: 'T-001, T-002') omits T-003 even though Global Constraints (lines 32-37) requires T-003's registration commit on tests/run-all.sh/tests/run-all.ps1/.github/workflows/test.yml to land before T-004's — a real ordering dependency on the shared registration files that the Blockers field does not encode. design.md (lines 273-277) separately frames T-003/T-004 as parallel-safe, but only for fixture non-collision, not for commit-ordering on the shared files. This is a missing blocker whose omission could cause the promised commit-serialization to fail during implementation.

SKIP: no bugfix or debugging task in scope. T-003 is a regression-locking suite-authoring task; its Goal (tasks.md lines 365-375) and Out of Scope (lines 448-449) explicitly disclaim re-fixing: 'the fix itself already landed upstream in 2d8c6a5... and is NOT re-implemented here'; 'Re-fixing the #143 gate contradiction' is listed as Out of Scope. No task in tasks.md is scoped as bugfix/debugging/incident remediation.

findings: SCOPE-DISJOINT (Major): T-003 and T-004 share Planned Files tests/run-all.sh, tests/run-all.ps1, .github/workflows/test.yml with no Blockers relationship between them, despite Global Constraints (tasks.md lines 32-37) requiring T-003's registration commit to land before T-004's.

DEPENDENCY-OVERLAP (Major): T-004's Blockers field (tasks.md line 600) omits T-003, leaving the Global Constraints' required commit-serialization on the shared registration files structurally unenforced.
